ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for cocktail prawn salad on a soft bun with dashi butter. FOODON_03315763 (shrimp salad food product) used as closest parent. Dashi butter component and bun not represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM (Crustaceans, cooked) used as closest parent. The composite dish includes salad dressing, dashi butter, and a soft bun which are not captured by this code alone.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Prawn dish) used as closest parent. Specific preparation with dashi butter and soft bun not represented in SNOMED CT.

serving.description: 1 serving (bun with prawn salad and dashi butter)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of a grilled handmade soft bun topped with cocktail prawn salad and a slice of dashi butter, weighing approximately 180 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Shrimp salad (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite) and bread roll components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for cocktail prawn salad with dashi butter on a soft bun in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked shrimp (FDC ID 175180), mayonnaise-based salad dressing (FDC ID 172237), and enriched dinner roll (FDC ID 168012), weighted to approximate a typical cocktail prawn salad bun. Dashi butter contribution estimated from butter (FDC ID 173410) and dashi stock (negligible macro contribution). Proportions estimated as approximately 40% prawn salad mixture, 50% soft bun, 10% dashi butter by weight. All values are estimates and should be verified against a laboratory analysis or HPB-registered entry if available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — soft white bread and mixed seafood salad analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cocktail prawn salad with dashi butter on a soft bun. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate source being a soft white bread roll (GI ~71 per University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by the protein and fat content of the prawn salad and dashi butter components, which are known to lower the composite GI of a mixed meal. The composite GI of approximately 52 is consistent with published mixed-meal GI values for protein-fat-enriched bread-based dishes. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low). GL per serving calculated as (52 × 42.3 g available carbohydrate per 180 g serving) / 100 = 22.0, rounded to 22.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 420 mg per 100 g; a single serving of 180 g provides approximately 756 mg sodium, representing about 33% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this dish due to its high sodium load from the salad dressing, dashi stock, and butter; consider requesting reduced-sodium preparation or limiting portion size to half a serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Composite GI of approximately 52 (low) and GL of approximately 22 per serving; the protein and fat from prawns and dashi butter moderate the glycaemic response, but the soft bun contributes a significant carbohydrate load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume this dish occasionally; the low composite GI is favourable, but the glycaemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 22, so portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables is advised to manage postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from dashi butter (approximately 3.1 g per 100 g) and dietary cholesterol from prawns (approximately 68 mg per 100 g) are relevant cardiovascular risk factors; sodium is also elevated.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the saturated fat from butter and the cholesterol from prawns; occasional consumption is acceptable within a heart-healthy dietary pattern, but frequent intake is not recommended given the combined saturated fat and sodium burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cocktail Prawn Salad with Dashi Butter on Soft Bun is a Singaporean café-style fusion dish served at Sinpopo Coffee, a nostalgic local café brand. The dish features cocktail prawns dressed in a creamy salad mixture, served on a grilled handmade soft bun, and finished with a slice of dashi butter — a compound butter infused with Japanese dashi stock that adds a savoury umami depth. The soft bun is lightly grilled to achieve a warm, slightly crisp exterior while remaining pillowy inside. The prawn salad component is typically made with small cooked cocktail prawns bound with mayonnaise-based dressing, providing a rich, creamy, and mildly sweet flavour profile. The dashi butter melts over the warm bun, imparting a subtle oceanic umami note that complements the sweetness of the prawns. The dish evokes the retro charm of old-school Singapore coffee shop culture reimagined in a modern café setting. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 198 kcal per 100 g, with 9.2 g protein from the prawns, 7.8 g fat largely from the butter and dressing, and 23.5 g carbohydrate from the soft bun. Sodium is elevated at approximately 420 mg per 100 g, making it a moderate-to-high sodium dish. The composite glycaemic index is estimated at 52 (low), moderated by the protein and fat content. Clinically, the dish is of concern for individuals managing hypertension due to its sodium content, and warrants portion awareness for those with Type 2 Diabetes given its moderate glycaemic load per serving. The dish contains crustaceans, gluten, dairy, eggs, and fish-derived ingredients (dashi), making it unsuitable for individuals with these allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by component-level composition using USDA SR Legacy data for cooked shrimp, mayonnaise-based dressing, enriched dinner roll, and butter, weighted by estimated proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on the dominant carbohydrate source moderated by protein and fat. All values carry meaningful uncertainty and should be verified against laboratory analysis or a registered HPB entry.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. All per_100g values are estimated composites; no single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish. The dish is served exclusively at Sinpopo Coffee - Funan (field survey data listing) and is not present in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or the food_index.json FD series. Dashi is a Japanese fish-based stock (typically made from kombu and katsuobushi/bonito flakes); its inclusion means the dish contains fish allergens and is unlikely to be halal-certified. The is_halal flag is set to false as dashi typically contains bonito (fish) and the halal status of the restaurant has not been confirmed. Sodium estimate of 420 mg per 100 g reflects the combined contribution of salad dressing, dashi stock, and butter; actual sodium may vary significantly by preparation. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; gi_category is 'low' per the threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low). GL per serving = (52 × 42.3) / 100 ≈ 22.0 g, where available carbohydrate per 180 g serving = 23.5 × 1.8 × 1.0 = 42.3 g. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and carry uncertainty. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used.
